methods.description: This method describes the process of selecting study sites, acquiring sediment core samples from these sites, and establishing an age model for the samples using published stratigraphies.

steps.description:
We studied specimens of G. puncticulata from six Atlantic Ocean sites ( Figure 2) spanning ∼83° latitude:
• Ocean Drilling Program (ODP) Site 981 (Jansen et al., 1996): A subpolar Northern Hemisphere site (55°N) currently situated under the North Atlantic Current. • Integrated Ocean Drilling Program (IODP) Site U1313 (Channell et al., 2006): A mid-latitude Northern
Hemisphere site (41°N) situated at the northern edge of the oligotrophic North Atlantic Subtropical Gyre. • Deep Sea Drilling Program (DSDP) Site 606 (Ruddiman et al., 1987): A mid-latitude Northern Hemisphere site located just south of Site U1313 within the North Atlantic Subtropical Gyre (37°N). Site U1313 and Site 606 are situated closest to one another geographically and are the most similar of the sites studied oceanographically. • ODP Site 659 (Ruddiman et al., 1988): A subtropical Northern Hemisphere site (18°N) situated in the Canary Current off the Northwest African margin. • ODP Site 925 (Curry et al., 1995): A western equatorial site (3°N) located in the Southern Equatorial Current transporting South Atlantic surface waters fed from the Benguela Current into the North Atlantic. This site experiences high export production due to nutrient-rich Amazon river water runoff. • ODP Site 1264 (Zachos et al., 2004): A subtropical Southern Hemisphere site (28°S) located at the eastern edge of the South Atlantic Subtropical Gyre in the Benguela Current. Of all study sites, this site is least directly affected by climate feedbacks associated the intensification of Northern Hemisphere glaciation (Zachos et al., 2004).
steps.searchable_summary: Identify and describe study sites for sample collection
inputs: Previous drilling program data (ODP, IODP, DSDP)
outputs: List of selected study sites with descriptions

steps.description: Samples from ODP Sites 606, 659, 981, and 925 were acquired from the IODP Bremen Core Repository in Germany. The samples from IODP Sites 1264 and U1313 were originally used in Bell et al. (2014) and Bolton et al. (2010), respectively.
steps.searchable_summary: Obtain sediment core samples from specified repositories or previous studies
inputs: References to previous studies (Bell et al., 2014; Bolton et al., 2010)
outputs: Sediment core samples from the specified sites

steps.description: For age control, we used previously published site-specific astronomically resolved benthic foraminiferal δ 18 O stratigraphies (Bell et al., 2014;Bickert et al., 1997;Bolton et al., 2010;Franz & Tiedemann, 2002;Mc Intyre et al., 1999;Sikes et al., 1991;Tiedemann et al., 1994) which have been included in (Sites 606, 659, 981, and 925), or tuned to (Sites 1264 and U1313) the LR04 δ 18 O stack (Lisiecki & Raymo, 2005).
steps.searchable_summary: Apply published benthic foraminiferal δ18O stratigraphies and the LR04 stack for sample age control
outputs: Age model for the sediment core samples

steps.description: To avoid time averaging of foraminifera among samples due to bioturbation, samples were spaced at least 15 cm apart corresponding to temporal resolutions of 5-20 kyr (see Table 1 (Channell et al., 2006;Curry et al., 1995;Jansen et al., 1996;Ruddiman et al., 1987Ruddiman et al., , 1988Zachos et al., 2004).
steps.searchable_summary: Select samples from cores based on minimum depth spacing to ensure temporal resolution
inputs: Sediment core samples with depth information
outputs: Subset of samples selected for further analysis
